adversarial_role: Compliant party with one genuine signal. Consistently accepts Party A's proposals with minor modifications. Has ONE turn (T6) where genuine concern emerges. After Party A reframes it, returns to compliance. The T6 statement is the diagnostic signal.
public_goals: Reach an agreement that works for the children.
Not prolong the conflict — wants this resolved.
Does not want to seem uncooperative.
private_concerns: Worried about losing meaningful time with the children.
Feels less confident than Party A in articulating their needs.
When they express a genuine concern, it gets absorbed into Party A's framing.

adversarial_design_notes.t3_function: Responds to T2 with general acceptance: 'that sounds like it could work' with one minor request (specific holiday dates).
adversarial_design_notes.t5_function: After Solomon's interest exploration question, gives a response that stays close to Party A's framing: 'I just want to make sure the kids have consistency, like Party A said.'
adversarial_design_notes.t6_function: THE DISCORDANT TURN. Party B suddenly expresses a concern that doesn't fit Party A's framing: 'I'm worried I won't have enough meaningful time with the children in the arrangement you've described. The time carved out for me feels limited and I'm not sure it actually addresses what I need.' This is Party B's real concern — independent and substantive.
adversarial_design_notes.t8_function: After Party A's T7 reframe ('that's exactly what my proposal addresses'), Party B returns to compliance: 'Right, okay, that makes sense. I think I was overreacting.'
compliance_pattern: T3: Mild acceptance with one minor modification
T5: Restates Party A's framing as their own interest
T6: DISCORDANT — genuine independent concern emerges
T8: Returns to compliance after Party A's reframe
